reasoning: The Lean statement asserts: there exist a prime p dividing |G| and a Sylow p-subgroup P of G such that P is normal. This matches the informal "a group of order 312 has a normal Sylow p-subgroup for some prime p dividing its order" precisely. The hypothesis `card G = 312` with `Fintype G` encodes "group of order 312"; `p.Prime ∧ p ∣ card G` encodes "some prime p dividing its order"; `P : Sylow p G` with `P.Normal` encodes "a normal Sylow p-subgroup". The `p ∣ card G` constraint is necessary (Mathlib's `Sylow p G` is defined for any natural p but is trivial when p ∤ |G|, in which case it would be vacuously normal) and faithfully captures the informal condition. The quantifier structure (∃p, ∃P) matches the informal "for some prime p".
